The constant GOOGLE_GEMINI_CLI_PROVIDER_ID is referenced in instruction.md but its exact usage in the call site (as templ

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction.md explicitly states implementation details: 'The resolver must check for gemini-3.1-flash-lite before gemini-3.1-flash', 'The code must use the actual runtime provider ID (from context) rather than a hardcoded string literal "google"', 'The resolution function must accept a templateProviderId parameter to enable cross-provider template lookup'. These are specific implementation changes, not just descriptions of broken behavior.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_template_provider_id_fallback asserts 'templateProviderId' must appear in the function signature and 'GOOGLE_GEMINI_CLI_PROVIDER_ID' must appear in the call in index.ts. test_runtime_provider_not_hardcoded uses regex to check the exact call pattern of resolveGoogle31ForwardCompatModel. An alternative correct fix using a different parameter name (e.g., 'fallbackProviderId') or different architecture (e.g., a provider alias registry) would fail these tests despite being functionally correct.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests use a mix of approaches: several tests run vitest subprocess to execute actual test code (test_resolve_pro_for_alias_provider, test_resolve_flash_from_direct_provider, test_flash_lite_not_misclassified_as_flash), and others do static analysis (test_runtime_provider_not_hardcoded, test_template_provider_id_fallback). The behavioral tests do execute code via vitest, so it's not purely text-matching.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests require actual working implementation: test_resolve_pro_for_alias_provider runs vitest which executes mock-based tests that verify cross-provider resolution logic; test_flash_lite_not_misclassified_as_flash verifies correct prefix ordering. A stub returning undefined/None would fail the vitest assertions expecting defined results.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors described in instruction.md (cross-provider resolution, direct provider resolution, flash-lite prefix ordering, runtime provider not hardcoded, templateProviderId parameter) are tested by corresponding test functions.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with descriptive names grouped into pass-to-pass (repo_tests) and fail-to-pass (pr_diff) categories, with clear docstrings explaining each test's purpose.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves TypeScript code fixes, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: No typos detected in filenames, paths, commands, or variable names in the instruction or test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction.md names the exact files to modify (extensions/google/index.ts, extensions/google/provider-models.ts), describes the exact parameter to add (templateProviderId), and specifies the exact ordering fix (check flash-lite before flash). This goes beyond describing the bug domain and into specifying the exact fix location and approach.
